title: Only Static Class Initializers Should Be Used
description: Non-static initializers, also known as instance initializers, are blocks of code within a class that are executed when an instance of the class is created. While these initializers may have some limited use cases, they are rarely used and can be confusing for most developers because they only run when new class instances are created. This rule enforces the use of only static class initializers.
recommendation: It is recommended to only use static class initializers to perform the common initialization logic for all objects of a class. This allows for a more concise and centralized manner of initializing instance variables, without the need to repeat the same initialization code in each constructor.
impact: Non-compliance with this rule can lead to confusion for developers when trying to understand the flow of code execution, potentially leading to introduction of bugs. It can also result in redundancy of initialization code if repeated in multiple constructors.

checks.message: It is recommended to only use static class initializers to perform the common initialization logic for all objects of a class. This allows for a more concise and centralized manner of initializing instance variables, without the need to repeat the same initialization code in each constructor.
